verses.text: David organizes a militia of twelve courses, each of twenty-four thousand, serving month by month through the year. The burden of defence is shared in orderly rotation. Good government spreads its load fairly, so the kingdom is guarded without crushing any.

David organizes a militia of twelve courses, each of twenty-four thousand, serving month by month through the year. The burden of defence is shared in orderly rotation. Good government spreads its load fairly, so the kingdom is guarded without crushing any.

Each monthly course is named with its captain. The arrangement is careful and complete. God honours order and shared service; the strength of a people lies in many ordered together, each in his appointed time.

Each monthly course is named with its captain. The arrangement is careful and complete. God honours order and shared service; the strength of a people lies in many ordered together, each in his appointed time.

The remaining courses and their commanders are listed, covering all twelve months. No season is left unguarded. Faithful service has its turns, and the work of many hands keeps the whole kingdom secure.

Rulers are appointed over each tribe. The whole nation is well led, tribe by tribe. God's people flourish under faithful oversight, with capable leaders set over every part.

David did not number those under twenty, recalling the wrath that came from his census, and trusting God's promise to make Israel 'like to the stars.' Chastened, he leans on the promise, not the count. Faith trusts God's word above its own reckonings.

Stewards are set over the king's treasures, fields, vineyards, olive trees, and herds. Every resource has a faithful overseer. Diligent management of what God gives, even in ordinary affairs, is a godly stewardship.

Stewards are set over the king's treasures, fields, vineyards, olive trees, and herds. Every resource has a faithful overseer. Diligent management of what God gives, even in ordinary affairs, is a godly stewardship.

More stewards oversee the flocks, camels, and asses — 'all these were the rulers of the substance which was king David's.' The practical and material had their keepers. Faithful work in earthly matters is honoured in God's record too.

David's counsellors are named — wise advisers, a scribe, the king's companion, and Joab over the army. He surrounded himself with counsel. Even a great leader needs wise advisers; safety lies in a multitude of counsellors.
